subjects_i_dislike: anything where there's only one right answer
academics.rigor_self_rating: Pretty intense — 2 APs and multiple honors
academics.above_and_beyond: Won the school's juried art show two years in a row. Got a piece selected for the CT Scholastic Art Awards regional showcase. Currently building a portfolio for college applications.
academics.approach_to_school: I care more about my art than my grades, but I keep grades up so my parents don't lose it.

performing_arts.performances: Played Beatrice in Much Ado About Nothing junior year (lead role). Stage managed the fall play sophomore year.
performing_arts.proud_of: I directed a one-act play I wrote myself for the spring showcase
performing_arts.pursue_in_college: Maybe minor in theater, not major

visual_arts.medium: Drawing (charcoal + ink), photography, some digital illustration
visual_arts.portfolio_pieces: 10-piece concentration on identity and bilingual childhood for AP Studio Art. Photography series on women in my family.
visual_arts.shows_publications: School juried show (won 2 years), CT Scholastic Art Awards regional showcase (Silver Key, 2024)
visual_arts.pursue_in_college: Yes — looking at BFA or BA + studio minor

things_built: Self-published a zine about being a first-gen Latina at a mostly-white high school — sold 80 copies at school for $5 each, donated proceeds to a local literacy nonprofit
Run an Instagram art account (@maya.draws.something) with 1,800 followers — I post weekly and have done 3 commission cycles
Co-designed the visuals for our school's mental health awareness week last year
clubs_leadership: Drama Club — member 4 years, Costume Designer junior year
work: Sold art commissions online — about $600 last year
activities.community_service: Volunteer at Domus art programming for kids — Wednesdays after school, 2 years.

identity.first_gen_college: Yes — my parents immigrated from the Dominican Republic. Neither has a 4-year degree.
identity.religious: Raised Catholic, not really practicing now
identity.other_background: First-gen Latina, queer, daughter of immigrants. These all show up in my art.

financial_reality.family_situation: Need significant financial aid. Probably need full ride or close at private schools. State schools are doable with grants.

what_i_want.dream_vibe: Somewhere I can be queer and Latina and an artist without anyone making it weird. NYC energy or college-town with culture.
what_i_want.city_vs_campus: City or town with a real art scene
what_i_want.distance_from_home: Road trip distance, but I'd consider further if it's the right school
what_i_want.size_preference: Small (under 2K) or medium (2K-10K) — I want professors who know my name
what_i_want.sports_culture: I want low-key — not a sports school
what_i_want.religious_affiliation: Doesn't matter, but not strictly religious
what_i_want.diversity: A lot — I'm done with being one of three Latina girls in my AP classes
what_i_want.weather: Actual winter is fine, mild is better
what_i_want.instant_no: Conservative campus or no Latine student community
what_i_want.instant_yes: Strong art program + queer-friendly + visible Latine community + financial aid

career_direction.today_pick: Some combination of art, writing, and storytelling — illustrator, graphic designer, or maybe art therapist
career_direction.miserable_in_job: Pure corporate environment where my work has to be safe and on-brand for someone else

real_talk.schools_should_know: I made my zine, my Instagram, and my art commissions while taking 2 APs and being VP of two clubs. I do the work.
real_talk.worry: Going to a school where I have to be the only Latina queer artist in the room again. And debt — I really can't take on a lot.
real_talk.success_in_4_years: Graduating from a school I felt seen at, with a portfolio I'm proud of, working in publishing or design or going to grad school for art therapy.
real_talk.anything_else: Don't just send me art school options. Some BFAs are great but liberal arts colleges with strong art programs might be better for me. Surprise me.
